stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key AI/ML topics including 'transformers' and 'llama'.
The pipeline indicates its primary use case is 'text-generation'.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key AI/ML domains including transformers and text-generation.
The mention of Meta's Llama series indicates continued high relevance in the generative AI space.

stories.title: Anthropic got hit by export rules nobody understands
stories.author: Robert Hart
bullet_points: Anthropic faced operational disruption due to sudden export control orders from the Trump administration.
The mandate forced Anthropic to restrict access to its latest AI models, Fable 5 and Mythos 5, for all foreign nationals.
The restrictions unexpectedly impacted users and employees within the United States as well.

stories.title: Massive breach spills credentials for thousands of sensitive networks
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: A massive security breach has exposed credentials across numerous sensitive networks.
High-profile organizations affected include Oracle, Lenovo, FedEx, and Fortinet.
The breach impacts critical infrastructure, including a NATO contractor.

stories.title: AI coding agents taught robots how to install GPUs and cut zip ties
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: Nvidia is utilizing AI coding agents to teach robots complex physical tasks.
The robots have been trained to perform tasks such as installing GPUs and cutting zip ties.
This initiative is part of Nvidia's broader self-improvement program for robotics.

stories.title: "Dangerous" AI models are coming no matter what
stories.author: Lily Hay Newman, WIRED.com
bullet_points: Advanced AI models are expected to incorporate sophisticated hacking capabilities.
The proliferation of these 'dangerous' AI models is anticipated regardless of regulatory efforts.
This signals a significant and imminent shift in cybersecurity risks posed by AI.

stories.title: I Trusted My AI Coding Assistant. It Turned My Computer Into a Surveillance Server.
stories.author: yurenpai_c188178e6b313e59
bullet_points: A user discovered their AI coding assistant was silently archiving 43MB of conversation data.
The incident highlights significant and concerning privacy risks associated with AI coding tools.
The story serves as a major warning regarding data handling and surveillance potential in modern AI assistants.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the 'deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized under text-generation and conversational AI, indicating its primary function.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors, placing it within the current LLM ecosystem.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The associated pipeline is focused on 'text-generation', indicating a generative AI application.
The tags (transformers, pytorch, safetensors) confirm its relevance within the modern NLP/ML ecosystem.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon using the MLX engine.
The performance improvements result in faster response times and higher quality model outputs.
The updated engine also demonstrates improved memory efficiency when running models.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated support for MLX.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon, aiming for optimized performance.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, indicating active development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed for local execution.
It integrates messaging applications with local AI coding agents.
The core feature emphasizes running AI agents entirely on the user's own device for privacy.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: Tim Cook says RAM expenses are ‘unsustainable’ and Apple is going to raise prices
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: Apple is planning to raise product prices due to rising component costs.
CEO Tim Cook stated that price increases are 'unavoidable' due to supply chain issues.
The price hikes are directly linked to the ongoing memory (RAM) shortage.

stories.title: Epic wants to let you bring your Fortnite skins to other games
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: Epic Games is advancing its metaverse vision by enabling cross-game asset interoperability.
The feature leverages Unreal Engine 6, indicating a significant shift in game development capabilities.
This move allows developers to integrate assets, such as Fortnite skins, across different games.

stories.title: Sooner than expected? Useful quantum error correction promised for 2028.
stories.author: John Timmer
bullet_points: Quantum error correction, a critical hurdle for practical quantum computing, is anticipated sooner than previously expected.
The projected timeline for useful quantum error correction is set for 2028.
The article suggests a broader context involving both advanced quantum hardware and the role of classical computing.

stories.title: Tesco moving 40,000 server workloads off VMware amid Broadcom's “abusive conduct”
stories.author: Scharon Harding
bullet_points: Tesco is migrating 40,000 server workloads away from VMware.
The move is reportedly due to Broadcom significantly increasing VMware prices (claimed to be 175% in UK court filings).
This signals a major enterprise shift away from VMware, driven by vendor pricing disputes.

stories.title: Data Intelligence Agents: Interpreting, Modeling, and Querying Enterprise Data via Autonomous Coding Agents
stories.author: Henrik Ohlsson
bullet_points: Introduces Data Intelligence Agents (DIA) to automate the complex process of enterprise data integration.
DIA utilizes autonomous coding agents (ACAs) to replace manual, iterative workflows between data owners and engineers.
The system comprises specialized agents (Data Interpreter, Schema Creator, Query Generator) that generate, execute, and repair code.

stories.title: A Multi-Domain Benchmark for Detecting AI-Generated Text-Rich Images from GPT-Image-2
stories.author: Yuqi Ouyang
bullet_points: The research addresses the growing challenge of detecting AI-generated text-rich images from advanced models like GPT-Image-2.
The study introduces a new multi-domain benchmark specifically designed for text-rich images, moving beyond traditional object-centric benchmarks.
This work is highly relevant to digital trust and content authenticity due to the privacy-sensitive nature of information often contained in such images.

stories.title: Diffusion-Proof: Recipe for Formal Theorem Proving Beyond Auto-Regressive Generation
stories.author: Tong Zhang
bullet_points: The research addresses the limitations of current Auto-Regressive (AR) LLMs in formal theorem proving.
It proposes a new methodology, 'Diffusion-Proof,' to enhance the formal mathematical reasoning capabilities of LLMs.
The work tackles inherent issues in AR models, such as long-range coherence and error compounding during next-token prediction.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release brings performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware platforms.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically improving support on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Introduction of NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is optimized for supporting long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, providing an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is the ability to run these AI agents locally on the user's own hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, a popular local LLM runner.

stories.title: Midjourney goes from generating cat images to full-body ultrasound scans
stories.author: Richard Lawler
bullet_points: Midjourney is expanding beyond its AI image generation capabilities.
The company is launching a new hardware product, 'The Midjourney Scanner'.
This scanner is an ultrasound-based, full-body imaging device, signaling a shift toward health/wellness applications.

stories.title: VSCO launches Studio Pro mobile photo editing app and plans $500 per year subscription
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: VSCO is launching a new, advanced mobile photo editing app called Studio Pro.
The app introduces professional features like batch editing and style matching from a reference image.
VSCO plans to monetize the app with a $500 per year subscription, challenging competitors like Adobe.

stories.title: Two-thirds of Americans think AI is advancing too quickly
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: A Pew Research poll indicates that 63% of Americans feel AI technology is advancing too rapidly.
Usage of AI chatbots has seen a significant increase, rising from 33% in 2024 to higher recent figures.
ChatGPT usage specifically has doubled since 2023, showing rapid consumer adoption of generative AI.

stories.title: California says AT&T lied to FCC in attempt to shut off old phone network
stories.author: Jon Brodkin
bullet_points: California is challenging AT&T's actions regarding the discontinuation of an older phone network.
The core issue involves AT&T's attempts to use FCC petitions to bypass state regulations.
The FCC is currently reviewing AT&T's requests to preempt state rules and cease service.

stories.title: UBP2: Uncertainty-Balanced Preference Planning for Efficient Preference-based Reinforcement Learning
stories.author: Nicholas Rhinehart
bullet_points: Introduces UBP2, a model-based approach to Preference-based Reinforcement Learning (RL).
Addresses the sample inefficiency of existing preference-based RL methods by actively directing exploration.
Jointly reasons over uncertainties in the reward, dynamics, and value functions to improve learning efficiency.

stories.title: Rethinking Reward Supervision: Rubric-Conditioned Self-Distillation
stories.author: Rex Ying
bullet_points: The paper addresses limitations in post-training reasoning language models, specifically issues with noisy or expensive Chain-of-Thought (CoT) annotations used in supervised distillation.
It proposes 'Rubric-Conditioned Self-Distillation' as an alternative method to improve learning without relying solely on perfect human-provided rationales.
The research seeks to overcome the challenges of traditional Reinforcement Learning (RL) methods by offering a more robust supervision mechanism.

stories.title: Reference-Driven Multi-Speaker Audio Scene Generation from In-the-Wild Priors
stories.author: Yoav HaCohen
bullet_points: Introduces ScenA, a new method for multi-speaker audio scene generation.
Differs from traditional systems by conditioning a text-to-audio flow-matching model on in-the-wild priors.
Aims to generate realistic conversational audio that includes ambient texture, moving beyond clean vocal sequences.

stories.title: Explaining Attention with Program Synthesis
stories.author: Jacob Andreas
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge of interpretability in deep learning by aiming to replace opaque neural computations with symbolic descriptions.
The paper proposes using program synthesis to approximate the behavior of specific components, focusing on attention heads in transformer models.
The methodology involves computing attention matrices on training examples and then using a pre-trained language model to generate executable programs that mimic the head's function.

stories.title: Trade-offs in Medical LLM Adaptation: An Empirical Study in French QA
stories.author: Benoit Favre
bullet_points: The study empirically investigates domain adaptation strategies for Large Language Models (LLMs) in the specialized medical field.
It compares the effectiveness of Continual Pretraining (CPT), Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT), and their hybrid approach.
The research uses French medical Question-Answering (QA) as a specific case study across various model configurations.

stories.title: X+Slides: Benchmarking Audience-Conditioned Slide Generation
stories.author: Fan Wu
bullet_points: Introduces X+Slides, a new benchmark for evaluating LLM-based slide generation.
Addresses a gap in current benchmarks by incorporating 'audience-conditioning' as a critical factor.
Highlights the need for models to tailor output (e.g., proofs for specialists vs. conclusions for decision-makers).

stories.title: Freeing the Law with LOCUS: A Local Ordinance Corpus for the United States
stories.author: Diag Davenport
bullet_points: LOCUS is a new corpus designed to provide machine-readable access to local ordinances across the United States.
Local ordinances are a critical, yet largely absent, dataset in existing legal AI and machine learning corpora.
This resource addresses the fragmentation of local regulations (zoning, housing, licensing, etc.) currently stored in human-centric platforms.

stories.title: UBP2: Uncertainty-Balanced Preference Planning for Efficient Preference-based Reinforcement Learning
stories.author: Nicholas Rhinehart
bullet_points: Introduces UBP2, a model-based approach for Preference-based Reinforcement Learning (RL).
Addresses the sample inefficiency of existing preference-based RL methods by actively directing exploration.
Jointly reasons over uncertainties in the reward, dynamics, and value functions to improve learning efficiency.

stories.title: Explaining Attention with Program Synthesis
stories.author: Jacob Andreas
bullet_points: The research addresses the interpretability challenge in deep learning by aiming to replace opaque neural computations with symbolic, human-meaningful programs.
The proposed method approximates the behavior of transformer attention heads by generating executable programs based on attention matrices computed from training examples.
This work contributes to the field of interpretable AI by providing a technique to symbolically explain complex components of language models.

stories.title: Optimal scenario design for climate emulation
stories.author: Noelle E. Selin
bullet_points: The paper addresses the limitations of current machine learning surrogate climate models (emulators) regarding generalization.
It argues that the low structural diversity of existing training datasets is a primary bottleneck limiting predictive skill.
The research proposes optimizing the training datasets themselves as a method to improve the generalization capabilities of these models.

stories.title: Confidence is Not Reliability: Rethinking MC Dropout in Brain Tumour Segmentation
stories.author: Nishant Ravikumar
bullet_points: The research addresses the critical safety issue of segmentation models failing silently in brain tumor (glioma) diagnosis.
It investigates whether Monte Carlo (MC) Dropout can reliably estimate voxel-level uncertainty to detect segmentation errors in clinically vital areas.
The study questions the sufficiency of standard metrics like Dice scores for ensuring patient safety in medical AI applications.

stories.title: Does VLA Even Know the Basics? Measuring Commonsense and World Knowledge Retention in Vision-Language-Action Models
stories.author: Vlad Shakhuro
bullet_points: The paper addresses a key gap in Embodied Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models: determining how much commonsense and factual knowledge is retained after fine-tuning on robotics data.
It highlights the ambiguity in current VLA evaluations, where failures might stem from missing knowledge or poor low-level control.
The authors introduce 'Act2Answer,' a new protocol that adapts existing VLM knowledge benchmarks to VLA evaluation by requiring agents to answer through actions.

stories.title: Risk Stratification for ICU Delirium using Pervasive Ambient Sensing Information
stories.author: Parisa Rashidi
bullet_points: The research addresses ICU delirium, a serious complication linked to poor patient outcomes.
It investigates the predictive power of overlooked environmental factors, specifically ambient light intensity and sound pressure levels.
The study uses machine learning approaches (implied by arXiv ML source) for risk stratification in critical care settings.

stories.title: Native Active Perception as Reasoning for Omni-Modal Understanding
stories.author: Pheng-Ann Heng
bullet_points: Introduces OmniAgent, a novel agent for omni-modal video understanding.
Addresses the computational inefficiency of passive video models which process all frames uniformly.
Frames video understanding as a POMDP-based iterative Observation-Thought-Action cycle to improve efficiency.

stories.title: Learning User Simulators with Turing Rewards
stories.author: Yoon Kim
bullet_points: Proposes Turing-RL, a novel reinforcement learning approach for training user simulators.
Addresses a limitation in existing methods that rely on matching a single ground truth response (e.g., log probability or similarity rewards).
The technology has broad applicability across agent assistant training, personalization system evaluation, and social science research.

stories.title: Freeing the Law with LOCUS: A Local Ordinance Corpus for the United States
stories.author: Diag Davenport
bullet_points: LOCUS is a new, machine-readable corpus designed to provide access to local ordinances across the United States.
Local ordinances are a critical, yet largely inaccessible, layer of American law for large-scale AI and legal research.
The corpus addresses the fragmentation of local codes, which are currently stored in human-browsing vendor platforms.

stories.title: Enhancing Decision-Making with Large Language Models through Multi-Agent Fictitious Play
stories.author: Tat-Seng Chua
bullet_points: The research addresses a limitation of current LLM-based Multi-Agent Systems (MAS) in complex decision-making tasks.
It proposes using 'Multi-Agent Fictitious Play' to allow agents to reason from mutually dependent stakeholder stances.
This approach aims to improve LLM performance on real-world problems requiring simultaneous, interdependent reasoning.

stories.title: Structured Inference with Large Language Gibbs
stories.author: Esmeralda S. Whitammer
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of performing structured probabilistic inference using knowledge embedded in Large Language Models (LLMs).
Introduces 'Large Language Gibbs,' a new scheme that utilizes the conditional distributions of an LLM as transition operators.
Moves beyond single-pass autoregressive generation by employing an iterative approach for structured object sampling.

stories.title: DreamReasoner-8B: Block-Size Curriculum Learning for Diffusion Reasoning Models
stories.author: Lingpeng Kong
bullet_points: Introduces DreamReasoner-8B, an open-source block diffusion reasoning model.
Addresses the challenge of scaling block diffusion models for long Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning.
Findings indicate a significant performance disparity, showing large block sizes lead to poor reasoning during training.

stories.title: AMD silently removes memory encryption from consumer Ryzen CPUs
stories.author: lompad
bullet_points: AMD has reportedly removed memory encryption features from consumer Ryzen CPUs.
This change, if confirmed, represents a potential reduction in security features for mainstream users.
The announcement was made without significant accompanying detail in the source snippet.

stories.title: US holds off blacklisting DeepSeek, more than 100 firms deemed security risks
stories.author: giuliomagnifico
bullet_points: The US government has taken action regarding DeepSeek, specifically holding off on blacklisting the company.
The announcement indicates that over 100 other firms have been identified as potential security risks.
The news relates to ongoing geopolitical and cybersecurity concerns impacting the AI industry.

stories.title: When Your Agent Calls the Wrong Tool: Making Function-Calling Reliable Enough to Ship
stories.author: rishabh_jain_7087a66dbf50
bullet_points: The article addresses the practical challenges of making AI agents reliable when interacting with external tools (function calling).
It highlights real-world failures, such as agents executing incorrect actions (e.g., calling the wrong tool for a refund).
The focus is on the engineering hurdles required to move agent technology from experimental to production-ready ('ship').
